query: What is the current convective severe weather pattern and forecast for the United States from late June 2026 through July 9 2026? Is there an active derecho or severe weather pattern expected for the Plains, Midwest, Ohio Valley in early July 2026?
answer:
I've completed my research. Here's a summary of the key findings on the convective severe weather pattern and forecast for late June through July 9, 2026:

## Bottom Line

**A classic derecho-favorable pattern is in place, but as of June 29, 2026, SPC has NOT issued any Moderate or Enhanced Risk for the near term.**

**Current pattern:**
- A major **heat dome** dominates the central/eastern U.S. with a textbook **"ring of fire"** MCS regime around its periphery — the prototypical synoptic setup for warm-season derechos.
- SPC's highest active outlooks top out at **Slight Risk (Day 1–2)** and **Marginal (Day 3, valid July 1–2)**. No Enhanced or Moderate is currently posted.
- The **Day 4–8 outlook** flags MCS/wind potential from Nebraska into the Dakotas (July 2–3) and the Upper Midwest (July 4), but with explicit **"predictability too low"** language.

**Derecho/severe signal:**
- AccuWeather explicitly warned of **derecho potential** with gusts up to 95 mph on the ridge's northern/northeastern flanks.
- SPC already issued an **Enhanced Risk** for the Central Plains on June 20, 2026, confirming the pattern is active.
- The ridge is forecast to **break down after July 4**, potentially boosting organized severe potential in the **July 5–9** window.

**Climatology relevant to the Moderate Risk question:**
- July is the **peak month for U.S. derechos** (~37% of warm-season events), in exactly the corridors now active (N. Plains→Upper Midwest, Corn Belt, Ohio Valley).
- Summer Moderate Risks (~14/yr total) are predominantly **wind-driven** and typically issued at **short lead (Day 1–2)**, essentially never in the Day 4–8 range.

I've delivered the full structured findings (key facts, 15 sources, and 10 quantitative data points) via the StructuredOutput tool. Per my role, I've reported current data and trajectory only and have not assigned a probability to whether SPC will issue a Moderate Risk.

sources.title: SPC Day 1 Convective Outlook
sources.snippet: Slight Risk for portions of the Northern Plains and Upper Midwest; large/very large hail and damaging winds, a couple tornadoes.

sources.title: SPC Day 2 Convective Outlook
sources.snippet: Slight Risk N. Wisconsin into Upper Peninsula of Michigan; Marginal in Northeast; damaging winds primary.

sources.title: SPC Day 3 Convective Outlook (valid Jul 1-2)
sources.snippet: Marginal Risk only; Central Plains into Upper Midwest and Northeast; MCS wind threat, no Enhanced risk.

sources.title: SPC Day 4-8 Severe Weather Outlook
sources.snippet: MCS development Nebraska into Dakotas Jul 2-3, Upper Midwest Jul 4; predictability too low elsewhere; confidence increasing.

sources.title: CPC Week-2 US Hazards Outlook (Jul 6-12)
sources.snippet: Extreme heat moderate risk; anomalous mid-level ridge persisting; no severe convective hazards highlighted for week-2.

sources.title: 'Ring of fire' thunderstorms to erupt around heat dome, bring rounds of severe weather
sources.snippet: Derechos possible on northern/northeastern flanks as heat dome strengthens; StormMax gusts to 95 mph; damaging hail, winds, tornadoes, flooding.

sources.title: SPC issues Enhanced Risk for Central Plains (June 20, 2026)
sources.snippet: Enhanced Risk for parts of Kansas/Nebraska June 20; very large hail, winds >75 mph, tornadoes possible.

sources.title: About SPC Convective Outlooks (risk category frequencies)
sources.snippet: Moderate ~14 days/yr, Enhanced ~80-85 days/yr, High 2-3 days/yr; High risk includes long-lived derecho with hurricane-force gusts.

sources.title: A Detailed Analysis of SPC Convective Outlook Patterns (Stang)
sources.snippet: Most moderate risks issued April-June; summer moderate risks driven by straight-line winds; ~10 moderate risk days/yr at Day 1+ lead; slight risks peak in July-August.

sources.title: Climatology of 1995-2024 US Warm Season Derecho Events (Shourd & Kaplan)
sources.snippet: 147 derechos 1995-2024 (~5/warm season); peak in July (36.7%); main corridors N. Plains/Upper Midwest, Corn Belt/Midwest, SE Great Plains; frequency increasing.

sources.title: A Climatology of Derecho-Producing MCSs (Bentley 1998)
sources.snippet: July is month of greatest derecho activity; favorable pattern = expansive anticyclone with warm moist air advected over ridge into Great Lakes (e.g., summer 1995).

sources.title: Updated Spatial Climatology of Derechos (Squitieri et al.)
sources.snippet: Warm-season derechos peak in June; corridors over southern plains, northern plains, Midwest into Ohio Valley; ~3/yr nationally under strict hurricane-force criteria.

sources.title: Heat wave and high humidity to blast eastern US ahead of July 4
sources.snippet: Heat indices 100-115°F; severe thunderstorm and flooding risks persist across northern Plains and Upper Midwest.

sources.title: Heat dome to bring 90-100° temperatures to 200 million in US
sources.snippet: Sprawling heat dome from Midwest to East from late June to early July; RealFeel above 110.

sources.title: Heat dome to bring dangerous temperatures to Ohio
sources.snippet: Central Ohio highs to 98°F; possible thunderstorms July 2, 3, 4, and 5.
